reviews.title: Good sound and good price
reviews.text: I’ve owned this for a couple years and still great sound. Very reliable. Others complement the full sound. It’s a loud, good quality sound and a good price.
reviews.author: DanaK

reviews.title: LOVE LOVE LOVE!!!
reviews.text: As always, Sound core does not disappoint and this is why I own 3 of their devices.
reviews.author: ShaIshMan

reviews.title: So much potential, but frustrating experiences
reviews.text:
I love a lot of things about this speaker, like the surround sound, shape (fits upside down in car cup holders & backpack water bottle pockets), the fact that it lights up! But this speaker has also been incredibly frustrating for me. 

Firstly, I have not gotten used to the skip and rewind function in the 2ish years that I've had it. I got used to the Soundcore Sport XL skip function in a few months so that I was able to consistently skip forward with two clicks of the button. But this Flare 2 man, sometimes I'm able to skip forward, but sometimes I press the button two times too fast and other times too slow. It's INCREDIBLY FRUSTRATING!

The other thing that's disappointing about it is the lighting features. The main reason why I wanted them was cause I don't like to shower in bright lights, especially at night. So I was planning on having some fun lights in the otherwise dark bathroom as a relaxing shower experience. I am able to use 1 of the several settings for this purpose. But ALL of the other settings are way too responsive to beats in the music and flash WAY too much. Even out in a party setting the degree of flashing to the music is unpleasant. I was really looking forward to using the multi colored light settings because they're SO PRETTY! >^^< But they're just completely unusable, unfortunately.
And the app doesn't allow me to dial it down or turn it off all the way. It's very annoying and disappointing, especially for a relatively expensive (for me) speaker.

One last thing, I would have very much appreciated having a place to attach carabineer or, even better, a strap I could wear around my wrist or attach to a carabineer or hook it to a floaty or paddle board.
reviews.author: Lunaru

reviews.title: Ups & Downs -- Too Many Downs
reviews.text: This is my experience:  The “Flare 2 Wireless Portable Waterproof Bluetooth Speaker” is certainly a good idea. However, music sounds quite muddy. Also, the buttons on top are way too hard to make out, even with their slightly raised surfaces. To me, the light show is silly. The battery life is phenomenal. Bluetooth connections are fast and easy. I didn't test its water resistance.
reviews.author: edpnw

reviews.title: Loud and portable
reviews.text: I use this primarily in the kitchen, but will also carry it around the yard when I'm gardening. Plenty loud enough, and while totally unnecessary, I enjoy the light effects.
reviews.author: Jayna

reviews.title: Great speaker / low bass
reviews.text: It's a great speaker but the bass is kinda low, to get the best out of it you gotta play with the eq. I found great settings online from a random user.

reviews.title: Anker offers High Quality and Reasonable Prices!
reviews.text: I've long been a user of Bluetooth speakers, JBL Flips, Bose Sound Link, UE BOOM, you name it I've tried them.  I have to say for the cost that like anything ANKER it exceeds expectations!  I bought two of these and the sound is crisp and clear!   It has a good battery life and achieves what one intends and you can link numerous to extend the distance and really make it a party!  I highly recommend this and anything by ANKER or their affiliate companies!

reviews.title: Good, but gimmicky
reviews.text: This has been my only Bluetooth speaker for years, and while I find the sound quality and software support from Soundcore to be good, the lighting is pretty gimmicky. Sometimes it's fun to use in certain settings, but it's not very good at syncing with the beat of the music, and that throws off the experience. I've set it up to turn off the lighting by default, and have been quite happy with it.

As far as bass and depth of sound, I bought the Motion X600 and it's clearer, but because I live in an apartment, the Flare 2 is more than enough and could get complaints made if I jammed it. 2 of these are better, 3 is best triangulated, but although I have 4, I only really use one.

Granted It's not perfect sound but for what it is and how much it can push, i doubt there's anything better out there at these price points. it's definitely way better than the Boom products I've had. Can't rate the battery life, i just plug it in while using it for its one and only purpose (singing in the shower lol)

reviews.title: Pretty Good Volume for the price but lacks clarity
reviews.text:
When its on sale (for me it was 59.99) its a value for money BT speaker. When its not on sale, you could probably re-evaluate your choices.

Pros - Pretty good volume. Aesthetic. Nice LED Color. Good Mid range sound. Extra Bass Volume gives it a thump. Ability to pair with another speaker.

Cons - Not Light, Stereo sound doesn't sound very stereo. Cannot reproduce lows and highs properly, mudded low/high sound. Pairing it with another Flare 2 doesn't give you the control of L-R. Runs out of battery life pretty quickly with the LED lights on.

reviews.title: Great speaker and battery life
reviews.text: I bought this for a beach trip and wasnt disappointed. Sound quality was really good. Id say on par with my sennheiser head phones. Has as much bass as you would expect out of a small speaker but the bass boost gives it a moderate boost without distortion. Sand and water didnt bother it. Battery life was also good. 50% volume with the leda on i got three full afternoons of use without needing to recharge. I only got one so i dont know how linking multiple speakers together sounds but i will eventually get some more. I bought this becaue the sonos i bought is a pain to use. One last thing. Range is like 30 ft max. Keeping my phone in direct line of sight made a difference but i kept it close for best use.

The bass is more fine than booming, until you pair 2 or more together, then it gets crazy. I bough 2 and then 2 more plus the new beat lights and my room is now a night club. The speaker is wonderful for all types of music. I tired EDM, Soul, Metal and Classical and it all sounds good. You won’t go wrong with this device.

Most bluetooth speakers I've come across are unidirectional - they sound best when the speaker cones are facing you. But this one projects in 360 degrees. It's particularly excellent when with company, set it in the middle of a table and you're all set. All bluetooth functionality works as expected. Bonus points for the very cool lights.
